text:
| [#210](https://github.com/pablof7z/tenex-edge/issues/210) | **Retrospective instrumentation** | `tenex-edge explain <handle>` + an `llm_calls` ledger — your "what was fed to the LLM?" question |

**#210** is the one that directly answers your motivating use case: a `tenex-edge explain <handle>` verb over artifact handles (`event:`, `hook:`, `llm:`, `txn:`, `session:@ts`), backed by a persisted `txn_receipts` table and an `llm_calls` ledger that captures the exact transcript slice + system prompt + model + raw response behind every distilled "current activity" — plus `replay` via trace-matching and a redaction policy for the user content in prompts. [...] | [#210](https://github.com/pablof7z/tenex-edge/issues/210) | **Retrospective instrumentation** | `tenex-edge explain <handle>` + `llm_calls` ledger — your "what was fed to the LLM?" question | [...] `LlmCallRow`/`NewLlmCall` (llm_calls.rs): `id: i64`, `session_id: String`, `window_hash: String`, `provider: String`, `model: String`, `system_prompt: String`, `transcript_slice: String`, `raw_response: String`, `parsed_title: Option<String>`, `parsed_activity: Option<String>`, `created_at: i64` (passed in, never clock-read). [...] `ReceiptRow`/`NewReceipt` (receipts.rs): `id: i64`, `surface: String` ("subscriptions"|"status"|"hook_context"), `transaction_id: i64`, `revision: i64`, `changed_summary: String` (JSON), `commands: String` (JSON array of `{kind,key,reason}`), `artifact_ref: Option<String>`, `created_at: i64`. [...] **Not bumped** — stayed at `1`. Both tables are pure additive `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` with no alteration to any existing table, and `schema.rs`'s own header comment establishes that additive tables don't require a version bump (the version gate exists to catch breaking/destructive changes, not additions). Flagging this per instructions in case a concurrent main-line change also wants the version bumped — reconcile if so.
